articles.title: Shop Front Scheme Progresses in Castleton with Ten Applications Received
articles.excerpt: The shop front scheme in Castleton is advancing, providing design advice and support to local business owners. Ten applications for grants have been submitted so far.
articles.content_html:
A shop front scheme is progressing in Castleton, offering design advice and support to business owners interested in applying for available grants. The initiative aims to enhance the appearance of local shops and encourage investment in the area.

So far, ten applications have been received from business owners looking to benefit from the scheme. The support provided includes guidance on design and application processes, helping to ensure that the improvements align with the overall aesthetic of the community.

The scheme is part of ongoing efforts to revitalise Castleton and promote local businesses. By improving shop fronts, the initiative seeks to attract more visitors and enhance the shopping experience for residents and visitors alike.

Further updates on the scheme and its impact on the local business landscape are expected as more applications are processed and improvements are made.

articles.title: Rochdale In Bloom Hosts North West in Bloom Judges
articles.excerpt: Rochdale In Bloom welcomed judges from North West in Bloom to the town centre this week, showcasing various local projects including Broadfield Slopes and the living roof at Number One Riverside.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale In Bloom welcomed judges from North West in Bloom to the town centre this week. The event showcased several local projects, including Broadfield Slopes, the living roof at Number One Riverside, Baillie Street Quarter, and the town hall square.

The judges assessed the efforts made by the community to enhance the local environment and promote biodiversity. The initiative aims to encourage residents and businesses to take pride in their surroundings.

This year's competition highlights the importance of green spaces and community involvement in maintaining a vibrant town centre. The judges' visit is part of the annual assessment of towns participating in the North West in Bloom competition.

Results from the competition will be announced later this year, with hopes that Rochdale will receive recognition for its efforts in improving the local area.

articles.title: Middleton councillors explore new measures to improve town-centre safety
articles.excerpt: Reform UK councillors in Middleton are considering a cycling dismount zone, a review of CCTV provision and wider community-safety measures aimed at making the town centre safer and more welcoming.
articles.summary: Councillors say they are gathering advice from highways, legal and community-safety officers before deciding whether new restrictions or improvements should be introduced in Middleton town centre.
articles.content_html:
Middleton councillors are exploring a range of measures designed to improve pedestrian safety and make the town centre a more welcoming place for residents, visitors and businesses.

Reform UK councillors elected in May said they have been examining whether a cycling dismount zone could be introduced in parts of Middleton town centre.

The proposal is intended to address concerns about anti-social behaviour involving bicycles while improving safety for people walking through busy areas.

Focus on practical and enforceable measures

Councillors said an important part of the work will be establishing whether any new restriction could be enforced effectively.

They are seeking advice from highways, legal and community-safety officers before any decision is made.

The group said it wants to ensure that any action introduced is practical, proportionate and capable of making a visible difference.

Review of CCTV provision

The councillors are also calling for a review of the town centre’s CCTV network.

They said residents should be able to understand how public-safety infrastructure is being used and whether it is helping to deter anti-social behaviour, support enforcement and improve confidence in the town centre.

The review could consider how cameras are monitored, how footage is used and whether the current network is delivering the intended outcomes.

Creating a welcoming town centre

The statement highlights concerns involving bicycles, e-bikes and e-scooters, while stressing the wider goal of creating a safe and positive environment for everyone.

Councillors said Middleton town centre should be a place where people feel comfortable spending time, shopping and supporting local businesses.

They are continuing to gather information before bringing forward any formal proposals.

Residents invited to contribute

Residents, businesses, pedestrians and cyclists are being invited to share constructive feedback as the work develops.

The councillors said local experiences will be important in shaping measures that balance pedestrian safety, accessibility and the needs of people travelling through the town centre.

This report is based on a public statement issued by Reform UK councillors in Rochdale borough regarding anti-social behaviour and safety in Middleton town centre. No final policy decision has yet been made.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports proposals and views expressed by elected councillors. No cycling dismount zone, CCTV change or other new restriction has yet been formally approved or introduced.
articles.right_to_reply: Rochdale Borough Council, Greater Manchester Police, Middleton residents, local businesses, cyclists and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Zen Internet Launches Big Moments Business Fund to Support UK Businesses
articles.excerpt: Zen Internet, based in Rochdale, has introduced the Big Moments Business Fund aimed at supporting the growth of UK businesses.
articles.content_html:
Zen Internet has launched the Big Moments Business Fund to assist growing businesses across the UK. The initiative aims to provide financial support to companies that are making a positive impact in their communities.

The fund is part of Zen Internet's commitment to fostering business development and innovation. It is designed to help businesses that may be facing challenges or looking to expand their operations.

Eligible businesses can apply for funding to support various projects, including technology upgrades, marketing initiatives, and community engagement activities.

This new fund reflects Zen Internet's ongoing dedication to supporting the local economy and contributing to the growth of the business sector in Rochdale and beyond.

articles.title: Rochdale Borough Council Announces New Initiatives for Local Development
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council has unveiled a series of new initiatives aimed at enhancing local development and community engagement in Spotland.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced new initiatives designed to promote local development and improve community engagement in the Spotland area.

The initiatives include plans for new community facilities and investment in local businesses. Council leaders stated that these measures aim to foster a more vibrant community.

Councillor Jane Smith, the portfolio holder for community development, highlighted the importance of involving residents in the decision-making process. She emphasised that feedback from local residents will be crucial in shaping these initiatives.

The council is expected to provide further details on the implementation of these initiatives in the coming weeks.

articles.title: Rochdale Organisations Recognised in LCEP Create Rochdale Shortlist
articles.excerpt: Several Rochdale organisations have been shortlisted for their contributions to the LCEP Create Rochdale initiative, highlighting their impactful work in the community.
articles.content_html:
A number of organisations from Rochdale have been recognised in the shortlist for the LCEP Create Rochdale initiative. The announcement celebrates the contributions made by local groups to the community.

Among those highlighted are Breaking Barriers Rochdale, M6 Theatre Company, and Tracing Steps, led by Martina Coggins. These organisations have been acknowledged for their significant efforts and projects that benefit the local area.

The LCEP Create Rochdale initiative aims to promote creativity and collaboration among local organisations, fostering a vibrant community spirit. The recognition of these groups underscores the importance of their work in enriching the cultural landscape of Rochdale.

The shortlisted organisations will be celebrated for their achievements, with further details expected to be announced in the coming weeks.

articles.title: Rochdale Pride Returns with Parade and Stalls This Saturday
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Pride will take place this Saturday, featuring a parade and stalls outside the Town Hall to celebrate the borough's LGBT+ communities.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Pride is set to return this Saturday, offering a vibrant celebration of the borough's LGBT+ communities. The event will feature a parade and various stalls outside the Town Hall, providing an opportunity for residents and visitors to come together in support of diversity and inclusion.

The festivities will commence at 12.30pm, with the parade scheduled to begin at 1pm. Participants and attendees can expect a lively atmosphere filled with music, entertainment, and community spirit.

This year's event aims to highlight the importance of acceptance and equality, fostering a sense of belonging for all. Local organisations and businesses will also be present, showcasing their support for the LGBT+ community.

Rochdale Pride promises to be a significant occasion for the borough, encouraging participation and celebration among residents. Further details about the event can be found on social media platforms.

articles.title: Car crashes into Amor restaurant in Bamford, causing damage
articles.excerpt: A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building's glass front shattered. No injuries were reported.
articles.content_html:
A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, July 13, leaving the building's glass front shattered. The incident occurred around midday, prompting an emergency response that included the Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service and Greater Manchester Police.

Pictures shared online showed the damage, with fire service tape cordoning off the area. The restaurant announced it would be closed for the evening due to the incident, stating on social media, "Due to unforeseen circumstances we will be closed tonight."

A spokesperson for Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service confirmed that firefighters were called to the scene shortly after 12.15pm. One fire engine from Rochdale station attended the incident, which involved a car crashing into the commercial building on Martlet Avenue. Fire crews worked to make the area safe before handing the scene over to police.

Fortunately, there were no reports of injuries following the crash. Greater Manchester Police were approached for a statement regarding the incident.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Classic cars and family dog show coming to Falinge Park
articles.excerpt: Falinge Park will host the Rochdale Classic Car Show and Amber Pets Dog Show on Sunday 19 July, with 15 fun dog classes and a Best in Show championship.
articles.summary: Falinge Park will host the Rochdale Classic Car Show and Amber Pets Dog Show on Sunday 19 July, with 15 fun dog classes and a Best in Show championship.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 12 July 2026 at 00:02. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Falinge Park will host the Rochdale Classic Car Show and Amber Pets Dog Show on Sunday 19 July, with 15 fun dog classes and a Best in Show championship.

Latest update

Classic vehicles and much-loved family pets will take centre stage at Falinge Park when the Rochdale Classic Car Show and Amber Pets Dog Show arrive on Sunday 19 July 2026 .

The public event, organised by Vintage Worx Community Park & Gardens, is scheduled to begin at 10am . The dog show will get underway at 11am , with 15 classes followed by the Best in Show championship at 2pm.

The opening classes include Best Puppy at 11am, Loveliest Lady at 11.10am, Handsome Hound at 11.20am, Child Handler at 11.30am and Golden Oldie at 11.40am.

Judging resumes at noon with Best Rescue, followed by Best Crossbreed, Best Pedigree, Dapper Dog and Gorgeous Girl.

Earlier developments

The afternoon programme includes Fancy Dress at 1pm and Best Kisser at 1.10pm. Judge’s Favourite classes will then be held for dogs weighing under 10kg, between 10kg and 20kg, and over 20kg.

The championship and Best in Show judging will begin at 2pm . The dog-show judge will be Nicky Clark .

Advance entries cost £2.50 per class and can be made through Amber Pets at 299 Edenfield Road, Cutgate Shopping Centre, Rochdale, OL11 5AQ. The shop can also be contacted on 01706 350378.

The event will be held at Falinge Park, Falinge Road, Rochdale, OL12 6JE .

Update timeline

  • 12 July 2026, 00:02: Classic cars and family dog show coming to Falinge Park
  • 11 July 2026, 23:52: Classic cars and family dog show coming to Falinge Park
articles.image_credit: Amber Pets / Vintage Worx Community Park & Gardens
articles.event_location: Falinge Park, Falinge Road, Rochdale, OL12 6JE
articles.source_name: Vintage Worx Community Park & Gardens and Amber Pets

articles.title: Jewellery and antiques roadshow coming to Whitworth
articles.excerpt: A jewellery and antiques roadshow will visit The Ashcroft in Whitworth on Wednesday 15 July, offering same-day cash for unwanted valuables.
articles.summary: We Buy Vintage will hold a roadshow at The Ashcroft on Market Street, Whitworth, from 10am until 3pm on Wednesday 15 July 2026.
articles.content_html:
A jewellery and antiques roadshow will visit Whitworth on Wednesday 15 July 2026.

We Buy Vintage will hold the event at The Ashcroft, Market Street, Whitworth, OL12 8DP, from 10am until 3pm.

Residents are invited to bring unwanted valuables, jewellery and antiques to the roadshow, where the company says items can be converted into same-day cash.

Event details

  • Date: Wednesday 15 July 2026
  • Time: 10am to 3pm
  • Location: The Ashcroft, Market Street, Whitworth, OL12 8DP
  • Organiser: We Buy Vintage

Anyone seeking further information can call 0800 669 6010 or visit the We Buy Vintage website.

Visitors should satisfy themselves that they understand any valuation or sale terms before agreeing to sell an item.
articles.image_alt: Poster for the We Buy Vintage jewellery and antiques roadshow in Whitworth
articles.image_caption: The roadshow will be held at The Ashcroft in Whitworth on Wednesday 15 July.
articles.event_location: The Ashcroft, Market Street, Whitworth, OL12 8DP

articles.title: Saturday Sessions bring live music to Rochdale town centre
articles.excerpt: Live acoustic performances are taking place on Saturdays in Rochdale town centre, giving visitors the chance to enjoy local music outside the Packer Street bars.
articles.summary: Live acoustic performances are taking place on Saturdays in Rochdale town centre, giving visitors the chance to enjoy local music outside the Packer Street bars.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 11 July 2026 at 23:45. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Live acoustic performances are taking place on Saturdays in Rochdale town centre, giving visitors the chance to enjoy local music outside the Packer Street bars.

Latest update

Live acoustic performances are taking place on Saturdays in Rochdale town centre, giving visitors the chance to enjoy local music outside the Packer Street bars.

Live acoustic music is coming to the heart of Rochdale town centre as the new Saturday Sessions programme gets under way.

The sessions will take place on Saturdays in Town Hall Square , outside the Packer Street bars, between 2pm and 4pm .

Organised by Rochdale Town Centre BID, the programme is designed to give residents and visitors a relaxed afternoon of live entertainment while supporting local performers and town-centre businesses.

Earlier developments

People are invited to meet friends, enjoy the atmosphere and listen to local musical talent in the recently transformed square.

The opening session features Rebecca Whitehead from Between the Vines , who will perform live acoustic music during the afternoon.

Further Saturday Sessions are expected to bring more live performances to Rochdale town centre over the coming weeks.

Update timeline

  • 11 July 2026, 23:45: Saturday Sessions bring live music to Rochdale town centre

The council encourages anyone needing assistance or information regarding estates to contact the department directly. This service aims to facilitate communication and support for local stakeholders.

articles.title: Rochdale Dumpling Day Celebrates Local Food and Culture
articles.excerpt: Hundreds gathered in Rochdale to celebrate community and culture at the third annual Dumpling Day, showcasing local food and traditions.
articles.content_html:
A large crowd attended the third annual Dumpling Day in Rochdale, celebrating the local community and its diverse culture. The event featured a variety of dumplings and traditional dishes, highlighting the culinary talents of local chefs and vendors.

Visitors enjoyed live music and cultural performances throughout the day. Local businesses participated by setting up stalls, offering food and crafts, contributing to the market feel of the event.

Organisers expressed gratitude to everyone who attended and supported the celebration. Dumpling Day has become a significant event in Rochdale's calendar, drawing people from surrounding areas.
